title: Set default Umask for `podman kube play`
body:
Fixes a bug where `podman kube play` fails to set a container's Umask to the default 0022, and sets it to 0000 instead.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Fixes a bug where `podman kube play` fails to set a container's Umask to the default 0022
```

comments.body: @Luap99 You're right, I screwed something up when testing on my machine and it fails to fix the bug I listed. However, this is still a bugfix regardless. Just doing a normal `podman create` will give you the correct default Umask. However, `podman kube play` does not.

Agreed this is a valid bug. The main issue here is not reading containers.conf so a test exactly for that would be good.
And the bz reference needs to be removed as I doubt that this fixes the linked bug.
I still don't see a CONTAINERS.CONF test?

> I still don't see a CONTAINERS.CONF test?

My thoughts were since exec currently isn't honoring any changes to containers.conf and we shouldn't rely on inspect, then that test wouldn't work

You should set the umask inside of a containers.conf  to something other then the default, and then run podman play kube with it, and then inspect the container to make sure it got the value from containers.conf.

CONTAINERS_CONF_OVERRIDE=PATHTO/containers.conf podman kube play
@rhatdan PTAL. rebased and added the test.

/lgtm

and happy CI. Leaving hold in case anyone else wants final review. Thanks @jakecorrenti.
